title: abies homolepis subsp. umbellata
intro: Abies homolepis subsp. umbellata, also known as Japanese fir, is a subspecies of fir tree native to Japan, characterized by its small, needle-like leaves and small cones. The medicinal properties of Japanese fir include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Japanese fir include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory problems, and aid in the recovery from illness due to its antimicrobial properties. Japanese fir can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
